query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on North Korea's 7th nuclear test status as of mid-2026:

## Key Findings

**No imminent test signs, but high technical readiness.** The dominant assessment is that North Korea *could* test on short notice, but the decision is political (Kim Jong Un's alone) and current dynamics favor restraint.

1. **Intelligence assessment (Nov 5, 2025):** South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency told lawmakers North Korea is "technically ready" to detonate at Punggye-ri on short notice, with preparations centered on **Tunnel No. 3** — refurbished to reduce detection signatures and compress warning time.

2. **Site status:** CSIS Beyond Parallel's most detailed public imagery read (Oct 2024) found the facility "well maintained" with prep largely complete, but **no indications of imminent test activity**. Tunnel No. 3 is the ready portal; Tunnels 1, 2, and 4 are collapsed/inactive. Notably, mid-2026 satellite reporting focused on **Yongbyon** (fissile material production), not the test site — I found no fresh mid-2026 Punggye-ri imagery report showing new activity.

3. **Restraining factors:** Analysts (The Diplomat, Dec 2025) assessed Pyongyang would suppress major provocations, including a 7th test, at least until after April 2026 to preserve diplomacy. June 2026 saw Trump–Kim communication and Trump–Xi reaffirming denuclearization commitments.

4. **Strategic pivot:** Rather than testing, North Korea appears focused on *institutionalizing* nuclear status — Kim's March 2026 "irreversible nuclear power" declaration and an expected 9th Party Congress "nuclear completion" statement rejecting CVID. Kim's recent statements make **no mention** of testing or Punggye-ri.

5. **Context:** Yongbyon is actively producing plutonium and expanding uranium enrichment (IAEA-confirmed April 2026), growing the fissile stockpile independent of any test.

The full structured report with sources and data points has been submitted.

sources.title: South Korean intelligence warns North Korea can conduct seventh nuclear test
sources.snippet: South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency told lawmakers (Nov 5, 2025) that North Korea can carry out a seventh nuclear test at Punggye-ri on short notice, pending Kim Jong Un's decision; activity centered on Tunnel No. 3.

sources.title: Update on Punggye-ri Amid Increased Tension
sources.snippet: Oct 4, 2024 imagery: facility well maintained, most preparations completed, no indications of imminent test preparations; Tunnel No. 3 primary; Tunnels 1, 2, 4 collapsed/inactive.

sources.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
sources.snippet: Kim Jong Un (March 23, 2026 SPA speech) vowed to consolidate 'absolutely irreversible status as a nuclear power' and demanded US drop denuclearization precondition; no mention of testing.

sources.title: Previewing North Korea's Grand Strategy for 2026
sources.snippet: Assessed North Korea would suppress major provocations including a 7th nuclear test until after April 2026; 9th Party Congress expected to declare 'nuclear completion' and reject CVID.

sources.title: Satellite imagery reveals increased activity at North Korean nuclear complex
sources.snippet: April 2026 satellite imagery of Yongbyon shows plutonium production and new uranium enrichment/fuel-fabrication construction; IAEA DG Grossi confirmed operational status April 15, 2026. No Punggye-ri mention.

sources.title: Press Statement of Kim Yo Jong, Department Director of C.C., WPK (June 18, 2026)
sources.snippet: Kim Yo Jong called Western denuclearization calls 'an empty goal that can never be realized.'

sources.title: Trump, Xi Said Committed to North Korea Denuclearization
sources.snippet: June 2026: Trump said he communicated with Kim Jong Un; Trump and Xi reportedly reaffirmed commitment to North Korean denuclearization.

sources.title: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
sources.snippet: North Korea's only known nuclear test site; six tests conducted Oct 2006, May 2009, Feb 2013, Jan 2016, Sep 2016, Sep 2017.

sources.title: North Korea's Nuclear Weapons and Missile Programs (CRS, updated April 29, 2026)
sources.snippet: CRS overview of North Korea's continuing nuclear arsenal advancement; six tests 2006-2017; readiness dependent on technical and political factors.
